reasoning: The Lean statement correctly formalizes each component of the informal claim. The local `abbrev Pro C := (Ind Cᵒᵖ)ᵒᵖ` is the standard categorical definition of the pro-completion. `FintypeCat` in Mathlib is the category of finite types with functions as morphisms, matching "finite sets and functions". `Profinite` in Mathlib is defined as compact Hausdorff totally disconnected spaces with continuous maps — which is exactly the category of Stone spaces described informally ("compact topological spaces in which distinct elements can be separated by clopen subsets"); for compact spaces, separability by clopens is equivalent to being totally disconnected and Hausdorff. The conclusion `Nonempty ((Pro FintypeCat) ≌ Profinite)` is the standard way to state the existence of a categorical equivalence as a proposition, matching "Pro(Set^fin) = Stone". Indeed, the Mathlib `Profinite` file lists this equivalence as a TODO. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
